AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR HOSSTON LA AND CROSSED INTO BOSSIER PARISH ENDING 5 MILES WEST OF PLAIN DEALING LA. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED ALONG ITS PATH. ONE HOME IN HOSSTON RECEIVED MINOR DAMAGE...WHILE SEVERAL HOMES IN THE PLAIN DEALING AREA RECEIVED SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE FROM THE TORNADO. THE TORNADO WAS 5 MILES LONG AND 550 YARDS AT ITS WIDEST. ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED WAS 115 MPH WHERE EF2 DAMAGE OCCURRED IN BOSSIER PARISH.

DAMAGE AT AVERY SCHOOLS... TREES DOWN... ROOF DAMAGE TO GYM... POWER OUT

AN EF0 TORNADO OCCURRED IN AVERY...BEGINNING ALONG CR 3326. THE TORNADO SNAPPED NUMEROUS TREES...DAMAGED THE ROOF OF THE SCHOOL GYM AND A DUGOUT AT THE BASEBALL FIELD...BEFORE LIFTING ON THE EAST SIDE OF TOWN. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 1.7 MILES...PATH WIDTH WAS 200 YDS...AND MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 80-85 MPH.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON LA COUPE ROAD...WHERE IT PEELED BACK THE METAL ROOF OF A BARN. THIS TORNADO ALSO SNAPPED NUMEROUS LARGE LIMBS...SOME OF WHICH LANDED ON A HOME BEFORE CONTINUING EAST ACROSS THE INTERNATIONAL PAPER PLANT. PORTIONS OF THE METAL WALLS AND ROOF WERE PEELED BACK AT THE IP PLANT...WITH SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED...ONE CAR FLIPPED OVER...AND NUMEROUS CAR WINDOWS BLOWN OUT. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST ACROSS SAMPLE ROAD BEFORE ENTERING WESTERN RED RIVER PARISH.

THIS IS A CONTINUATION OF THE EF-1 TORNADO FROM DESOTO PARISH. THIS TORNADO CONTINUED EAST ACROSS MAINLY OPEN PASTURE NORTH OF HIGHWAY 509...SNAPPING SEVERAL TREES AS IT CROSSED HIGHWAY 1. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST EAST OF HIGHWAY 1 BEFORE CROSSING THE RED RIVER. THE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 350 YARDS...WITH A TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF 6 MILES.

COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTS A BUILDING DESTROYED...2 ROLLED OVER 18 WHEELERS...AND DAMAGE TO A CEFCO GAS STATION AT THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 37 AND INTERSTATE 30.

A LOCALIZED DOWNBURST AFFECTED A SMALL AREA ALONG INTERSTATE 30 ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF MOUNT VERNON...PRODUCING AN ESTIMATED 70-80 MPH WINDS. DAMAGE CONSISTED IN THE SIGNIFICANT LOSS OF A ROOF OF A POORLY CONSTRUCTED SMALL BUSINESS AND A PARTIAL COLLAPSE OF THE EXTERIOR WALLS. A NEARBY BANK ALSO EXPERIENCED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE.
TREE DOWN ON HWY 37 AT IRON STOB ROAD

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF FM 852 NEAR THE BISON ROAD AND FM 852 INTERSECTION. THE TORNADO MOVED EAST CROSSING FM 852 AND PASSING ALONG ARMADILLO ROAD NORTH OF LAKE GILMER AND THEN CROSSING CHEROKEE TRACE...REACHING HIGHWAY 271 NEAR ANTELOPE ROAD BEFORE LIFTING. THE TORNADO CAUSED DAMAGE TO NUMEROUS TREES BY SNAPPING AND UPROOTING THEM ALONG THE PATH. SOME HOMES SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH DUE TO FALLEN TREES. SEVERAL BARNS HAD SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH. O
POWER LINES DOWN
TREES DOWN... BARN BLOWN DOWN POWER LINES DOWN NORTH OF LEESBURG BETWEEN LEESBURG AND LAKE BOB SANDLIN... ROADS BLOCKED

*** 1 FATAL, 4 INJ *** AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF THE ARDEN COMMUNITY NEAR HIGHWAY 32... WHERE IT SNAPPED AN UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES. THE TORNADO THEN DESTROYED A NEARBY MOBILE HOME... KILLING ONE 33 YEAR OLD MALE AND CRITICALLY INJURING A 34 YEAR OLD FEMALE. THREE CHILDREN WERE ALSO INJURED. ANOTHER NEARBY MOBILE HOME WAS DAMAGED AS THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST...LIFTING AS IT CROSSED CR 16. MAX WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE BETWEEN 120-130 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 2.3 MILES WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 400 YARDS.

AN EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF CR 108...WHERE IT SNAPPED A NUMBER OF TREES AND DESTROYED A BARN. THE ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS WERE 65-75 MPH. THE TORNADO PATH LENGTH WAS 0.1 MILES AND HAD A PATH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 4.5 MILES SOUTH OF MANSFIELD JUST EAST OF HIGHWAY 171. THE TORNADO MOVED EAST ALONG A 0.9-MILE TRACK AND LIFTED AT THE INTERSECTION OF LA 513 AND OXFORD CUTOFF ROAD. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 95-105 MPH WITH THE TORNADO REACHING 200 YARDS AT ITS WIDEST POINT. DAMAGE MAINLY CONSISTED OF UPROOTED AND SNAPPED TREES.

AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 3.5 MILES WEST OF THE CITY LIMITS OF WEST MONROE NEAR THE 4000 BLOCK OF NEW NATCHITOCHES ROAD. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. THE TORNADO MOVED EAST NORTHEAST ON A 9-MILE TRACK THROUGH WEST MONROE AND MONROE CAUSING DAMAGE TO NUMEROUS HOMES AND BUSINESSES...IN ADDITION TO WEST MONROE HIGH SCHOOL. THE TORNADO WAS 300 YARDS AT ITS WIDEST POINT WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 125 MPH.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG CAPLES ROAD NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF WESTERN MURRAY CIRCLE...SNAPPING NUMEROUS TREES AS IT CONTINUED NORTHEAST ACROSS EASTERN MURRAY CIRCLE AND GARDNER ROAD. SEVERAL HOMES ON MURRAY CIRCLE AND GARDNER ROAD SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE FROM FALLING TREES...BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED AS IT MOVED ACROSS THE SOUTHWEST SIDE OF CHENIERE LAKE. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED BETWEEN 90-100 MPH. THE TORNADO PATH LENGTH WAS 0.8 MILES...WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 225 YARDS.
